questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the environment?
questions.explanation: While technology and culture influence the environment, they are not inherent components like biotic and abiotic factors.

questions.question: Biotic factors encompass:
questions.explanation: Biotic factors are all the living organisms within an environment.

questions.question: Abiotic factors primarily consist of:
options: B) Non-living physical and chemical elements
C) The flow of energy through an ecosystem
D) The cycling of nutrients within an environment
questions.explanation: Abiotic factors are the non-living components that influence the environment.

questions.question: The term 'ecosystem' refers to:
options: A) A group of organisms of the same species living together
B) A community of interacting organisms and their physical environment
C) A single organism interacting with its surroundings
D) The Earth as a whole
questions.explanation: An ecosystem encompasses both living (biotic) and non-living (abiotic) components interacting as a functional unit.

questions.question: Understanding the components of the environment is crucial for:
questions.explanation: Knowing how the environment functions helps us address various environmental challenges.
